findings.title: Stopped workers can return an unrelated default log
findings.impact: When `find_worker_pid` returns `None`, `run` still calls `resolve_log_path(pid)`. With no process argv available, `resolve_log_path` falls back to default config values and `datasystem_worker.INFO.log`, so `dscli logs -w <addr>` can succeed while printing a different worker's default log or a stale package-default path instead of the requested worker's log.
findings.fix: Do not tail a guessed log when the worker process is absent; either fail with an actionable error, or require a config/log path source that can deterministically map the requested address to the worker's actual log_dir/log_filename.
